hybrid_candidates.name: scd_sel_once_a0999_biaslink_commit
hybrid_candidates.description: R58 + once-per-static-phase gentle SCD on the five protected/targeted selected scale states during iter2 only.
hybrid_candidates.rationale: Safest first hybrid: only trim nav/bias coupling into the exact R58-selected states, once per stop, with optimal-style mild alpha.

hybrid_candidates.name: scd_sel_once_a0998_biaslink_commit
hybrid_candidates.description: Same as candidate A, but a notch stronger alpha.
hybrid_candidates.rationale: Tests whether R58 still has a little room for dKg_xx/max repair if the selected-state coupling cut is made slightly stronger.

hybrid_candidates.name: scd_sel_once_a0999_navonly_commit
hybrid_candidates.description: Same gentle selected-state decay, but preserve bias↔target coupling and only trim nav↔target leakage.
hybrid_candidates.rationale: Checks whether the hybrid is safer if it cuts only the ZUPT/nav noise pipeline while leaving bias observability routes untouched.

hybrid_candidates.name: scd_scale_once_a0999_biaslink_commit
hybrid_candidates.description: Expand the once-per-phase cut from selected states to the full dKg/dKa block, still keeping Ka2/lever outside the decay target.
hybrid_candidates.rationale: Tests whether the hybrid helps more if the SCD idea covers the whole scale/misalignment block while still protecting the lever terms R58 already holds well.

hybrid_candidates.name: scd_sel_repeat_a09998_biaslink_commit
hybrid_candidates.description: Ultra-gentle repeated post-transition decay on selected states during iter2 only.
hybrid_candidates.rationale: Checks the other transition style: instead of one cut per stop, apply a nearly no-op repeated suppression after the grace period.
